title: Rule
description: A Rule defines a constraint on a Capability — either who is permitted to perform it (type: access) or what conditions must be satisfied before it executes (type: condition). Rules are checked before execution.

capability.description: The Capability this rule applies to, expressed as Resource.Action.

description.description: A human-readable explanation of what this rule enforces.

type.description: The kind of rule. 'access' controls who can perform the capability; 'condition' defines business conditions that must be satisfied.

allow.description: The Actors permitted to perform this capability. Used when type is 'access'. An Actor matching any entry is allowed; all properties within a single entry must match together (AND). Cross-entry semantics is OR.

role.description: A named role that is permitted.

ownership.description: When true, the Actor must be the owner of the Resource instance.

flags.description:
Feature flags that must all be enabled for this allow entry to match. An entry matches iff its role + ownership pass AND every named flag is currently enabled in the runtime flag source (env var / LaunchDarkly / Unleash — whatever the cell adapter wires up). Flags are free-form strings; there is deliberately no separate flag registry or dna-validator cross-check in this first cut, so typos surface at runtime rather than validate time.

A feature flag is just another form of access control around a capability — an always-off flag is equivalent to `allow: []`, an on-for-role-X flag is equivalent to adding a role entry. Pure kill-switch: `allow: [{ "flags": ["enabled"] }]` grants access iff the `enabled` flag is on.

conditions.description: The conditions that must all be satisfied for the capability to proceed. Used when type is 'condition'.
